bet_override: Hard stop: this trend fails India price band or climate fit — signal strength is irrelevant.

india_fit.price_band_reason: Velvet palazzo suits can be produced at ₹399–₹1,499 but velvet fabric + tailoring complexity may compress margins; doable but tight.
india_fit.climate_fit_reason: Velvet is heavy and warm; unsuitable for India's heat and humidity except winter months (Nov–Jan) in metros.
india_fit.cultural_fit_reason: Palazzo suits with kurta-style tops align with modest Indian sensibilities; velvet adds festive prestige appeal.
india_fit.value_fashion_fit_reason: Meesho/value-fashion customers favor seasonality and repeat categories; velvet palazzo is niche and seasonal, limiting annual relevance.

claude_synthesis.convergence_summary: All signals—Google Trends flat, marketplace presence weak (10 listings, no launches, 3 discounts), zero news coverage, minimal social buzz—converge on low baseline demand with no growth trajectory.
claude_synthesis.signal_agreement: Demand and buzz signals agree: both show flat/weak interest with no momentum.
claude_synthesis.bet_reasoning: The weighted demand score is only 1.2/2.5, reflecting stagnant Google Trends interest and shallow marketplace commitment (10 listings suggest testing, not confidence). The absence of new launches and presence of discount signals indicate existing stock is not moving—a red flag for a niche, seasonal product. For a value-fashion buyer with limited shelf space, this suggests low velocity and inventory risk.
claude_synthesis.skepticism_flag: Google Trends data showing 0/100 current interest may mask localized seasonal demand in Tier 1 winter markets (Delhi, Mumbai Nov–Jan), where velvet trends could spike undetected in this national aggregate; Myntra's 10 listings may undercount smaller players and Meesho resellers who drive volume in value fashion.
